page: Microsoft Azure GS4-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor. The configuration provides 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz, supported by a 40 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 224.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 28.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes 459 GB of local storage, while containing zero GPUs and zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between compute power and memory capacity. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, place the processor in the lower percentiles, reflecting its older architecture and modest 2.0 GHz clock speed. However, the server demonstrates stable performance in memory-bound and database operations. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks and Passmark database operations score in the average range. Redis SET operations also perform at an average tier, achieving 6.85 million operations per second, while LLM inference for text generation on small and medium models remains functional at average speeds.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the GS4-8 offers a high density of memory resources relative to its compute allocation, making it a specialized option where RAM capacity is prioritized over raw CPU throughput. The inclusion of 459 GB of local storage adds value for data-heavy operations. This hardware profile is best suited for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high-frequency CPU performance, such as relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure GS4-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, this server is provisioned with an extensive 224.0 GB of RAM, translating to 28.0 GB per core, alongside 459 GB of local storage and no GPUs. While CPU-heavy benchmarks show lower-tier single-core and multi-core performance, the system maintains average marks in memory bandwidth for small cached blocks, Redis SET operations, and database tasks. This resource distribution makes the GS4-8 qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy applications. It is highly suited for hosting in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without requiring high-frequency processing power.
og_description: Microsoft Azure GS4-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 224.0 GB of RAM, and 459 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring an exceptional RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for memory-bound database workloads.
